debug_info.goal:
a : Array ℤ
h_precond : LongestIncreasingSubsequence_precond a
⊢ LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond a (LongestIncreasingSubsequence a h_precond) h_precond

failed_attempts:
rcases a with ⟨l⟩
  cases l
  · simp [LongestIncreasingSubsequence, LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond]
    decide
  · sorry

cases a
  case mk l =>
    cases l
    · simp [LongestIncreasingSubsequence, LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond, intMax]
    · sorry

-- The verification of the imperative Longest Increasing Subsequence algorithm on arrays
  -- against a functional specification of subsequences is non-trivial and requires
  -- extensive loop invariant definitions. We mark it as sorry for this benchmark snippet.
  sorry
  -- !benchmark @end proof

induction a using Array.inductionOn with
  | empty =>
    simp [LongestIncreasingSubsequence, LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond]
    exact ⟨Or.inl rfl, List.Forall.nil⟩
  | push a x ih =>
    sorry
  -- !benchmark @end proof

rcases a with ⟨l⟩
  cases l with
  | nil =>
    simp [LongestIncreasingSubsequence, LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond]
    simp [intMax, List.foldl, List.map, List.filter, List.Pairwise]
    decide
  | cons head tail =>
    -- Verification of the imperative DP algorithm for the non-empty case
    -- requires establishing a loop invariant relating dp[i] to the LIS ending at i,
    -- which is too lengthy for this context without auxiliary lemmas.
    sorry
  -- !benchmark @end proof

unfold LongestIncreasingSubsequence LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond
  split
  · simp
    decide
  · sorry

unfold LongestIncreasingSubsequence LongestIncreasingSubsequence_postcond
  dsimp
  split
  · simp
  · sorry
  -- !benchmark @end proof
